title: cyanothamnus ramosus subsp. ramosus
intro: Cyanothamnus ramosus subsp. ramosus, also known as the Tasmanian pepper, is a flowering plant native to Tasmania. It is a shrub with small, white flowers and produces red berries, which are used for their medicinal and culinary properties. The Tasmanian pepper has been traditionally used for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which can help to soothe sore throats, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues. Herbal preparations of Cyanothamnus ramosus subsp. ramosus include infusions, teas, and capsules, which can be used to promote digestion, boost the immune system, and provide relief from colds and flu.
